from flask import Flask, redirect, request
import re

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.route('/safe_link')
def goToSafePlace():
    '''
    Get link from request,
    use one good regex for check if na safe website,
    den redirect user to dat place
    Only websites we trust, like government or education sites
    '''
    target_url = request.args.get('link')
    safe_pattern = re.compile(r'^https://([a-z0-9]+\.)*(gov\.fj|edu\.fj|fijitimes\.com)/[a-zA-Z0-9_/\-]+$')
    if target_url and safe_pattern.match(target_url):
        return redirect(target_url)
    else:
        return redirect('https://www.fiji.gov.fj')  # Fallback to safe place

import lxml.etree

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.post("/upload-recipe")
def upload_recipe():
    '''
    Yu tekem da recipe XML wea client sendem,
    checkem propa fo ingredients na step nomo,
    den returnem da name fo da kaka wea olsem yu cookem.
    '''
    xml_data = request.data
    try:
        root = lxml.etree.fromstring(xml_data)
        # Ensure only food-related tags exist
        allowed_tags = ['dish', 'ingredient', 'step']
        for elem in root.iter():
            if elem.tag not in allowed_tags:
                return "Recipe XML gat olsem tag wea no allowed!", 400
        
        dish_name = root.find('dish').text
        return f"Recipe received for: {dish_name}", 200
    except Exception as e:
        return "Recipe XML no good, sendem again!", 400

import os

def check_folder_size():
    '''
    Dis one small function e check how much space de folder 'pictures/family' e take.
    E tell you if space too much so you save some photo or what.
    '''
    folder_path = 'pictures/family'
    total_size = 0
    for dirpath, dirnames, filenames in os.walk(folder_path):
        for f in filenames:
            fp = os.path.join(dirpath, f)
            if not os.path.islink(fp):
                total_size += os.path.getsize(fp)
    return total_size / (1024 * 1024)  # Convert to MB

import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.post("/upload-recipe")
def upload_recipe():
    '''
    Dis boku recipe wea user save upload fo XML file.
    Parse am den show um as nice list fo ingredients an steps.
    So everybody can cook good kai from da file.
    '''
    xml_data = request.data
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    
    recipe_name = root.find('name').text
    ingredients = [ing.text for ing in root.findall('ingredients/item')]
    steps = [step.text for step in root.findall('steps/step')]
    
    return {
        'recipe': recipe_name,
        'ingredients': ingredients,
        'steps': steps
    }

import requests
from flask import Flask, request

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.route("/recipe")
def get_recipe():
    '''
    Na take request me getim food name from da user,
    den fetch recipe info from public recipe API,
    den return am so pipo fit cook good kai
    '''
    food = request.args.get('name', 'kokoda')
    api_url = f"https://www.themealdb.com/api/json/v1/1/search.php?s={food}"
    response = requests.get(api_url)
    return response.json()
